modulus<u64>

Translation status: 简体中文 reader-locale proof. Code fences render through the zh-Hans pipeline; prose is canonical Latin.

Unsigned 64-bit modular-word arithmetic and bit-pattern edges.

Syntax: modulus<u64>

Category

arithmetic

Related

  • numerus
  • ¬
  • ⇐
  • ⇒

Examples

examples/corpus/operatores/modular-word-u64.fab (canonical · operator-group)

Unsigned 64-bit modular-word arithmetic and bit-pattern edges.

# operatores/modular-word-u64 — modulus<u64> edge vectors
#
# MODULAR WORD CONTRACT:
#   modulus<u64> is an unsigned 64-bit modular word. Addition, subtraction,
#   multiplication, negation, bitwise operations, and shifts use u64 wrapping.
#   Division and remainder by zero fail.

incipit {
    varia modulus<u64> max ← 18446744073709551615
    fixum modulus<u64> one ← 1
    varia modulus<u64> zero ← 0
    fixum numerus<u64> converted ← one ↦ numerus<u64>

    nota max + one
    nota zero - one
    nota max * 2
    nota -one
    nota ¬zero
    nota one ⇐ 64
    nota max ⇒ 64
    nota max > zero

    # Carrier-mix equality: conversions and arithmetic results compare by
    # value against literals (review regression).
    fixum modulus<u64> uno ← 1 ↦ modulus<u64>
    nota one ≡ uno
    nota one ≡ one + 0

    max ⊕
    zero ⊖
    nota max
    nota zero

    fixum modulus<u64> methodSum ← max.addita(one)
    fixum modulus<u64> methodShift ← max.sinistrata(64)
    varia modulus<u64> methodAcc ← 0
    methodAcc.adde(one)
    methodAcc.sinistra(64)
    nota converted
    nota methodSum
    nota methodShift
    nota methodAcc

    fixum lista<modulus<u64>> words ← [one, max]
    nota words.accipe(1) vel (0 ∷ modulus<u64>)

    varia lista<modulus<u64>> sorted ← [18446744073709551615, 3, 10, 2]
    sorted.ordina()
    nota sorted
}

Expected output:

0
18446744073709551615
18446744073709551614
18446744073709551615
18446744073709551615
1
18446744073709551615
verum
verum
verum
0
18446744073709551615
1
1
0
1
0
[2, 3, 10, 18446744073709551615]
Sources
